target.name: Simulated ticket priority normalizer template submission

reviewer.relationship: First-party review of a repository-owned simulated community submission

domains.name: security
domains.notes: The transform bounds the raw untrusted subject before trimming or returning a reduced output, collapses malformed values and hostile accessor or Proxy failures to one fixed non-reflective message, calls no capabilities, denies egress, and rejects undeclared hooks. The packet checker scans every bounded regular file in the closed packet tree, including unreferenced artifacts, and rejects sensitive metadata, digest-bound source, and evidence including labelled or unlabelled raw account identifiers, private-host URLs across URL schemes, local file URLs, Unix and Windows machine-local paths, null provenance revisions, incomplete source digest maps, source drift, path escape, symlinks, package-manager control files, package-manager settings, install lifecycle scripts, and pre/post test wrappers, non-public repository and egress hosts, GitHub browser URLs, unrelated approvals, and unsafe guarantee language without reflecting submitted values. It also requires the packet-owned SECURITY.md so generic evidence cannot replace the dedicated trust-boundary guidance. Digest-map path keys may contain legitimate security vocabulary, while every referenced source file still receives the same content scan. Source digests remain enforceable after squash while available Git objects receive an additional content check, and the E2E disables lifecycle scripts and pnpmfile hooks while requiring offline installation.

domains.name: compatibility
domains.notes: The packet pins exact tested package versions, requires manifest and plugin SDK dependencies to equal the tested version, records a bounded SDK range and hook contract, uses strict ESM TypeScript, and is exercised against freshly packed public package contents. The package test script is canonical and the E2E directly runs the required digest-bound behavior-test file. The E2E discovers every submission directory rather than naming one packet and compares manifest hook, capabilities, configuration keys, and egress with packet metadata.

domains.name: operations
domains.notes: The handler is side-effect free, bounded by fixed input length and manifest limits, has no retry or external service behavior, and is continuously built, tested, bundled, and audited in an accountless lane. Its canonical build emits the manifest and bundle that the documented audit command consumes.

domains.name: documentation
domains.notes: The packet records its purpose, immutable source, hook, capability and egress boundary, canonical verification commands, evidence, security note, review record, and explicit non-guarantees.

domains.name: license
domains.notes: The simulated source is repository-owned, declares Apache-2.0 consistently with the repository, and contains no vendored code, assets, runtime dependencies, or generated binary artifacts.

unverified.item: Independent review by a community contributor
unverified.reason: This is an explicitly first-party simulation; no external contributor identity or endorsement is claimed.

unverified.item: Public npm installation and live Cloudflare execution
unverified.reason: The accountless E2E uses packed repository packages and does not access credentials or a live provider environment.

limitations: The decision covers only the listed source and evidence digests; the baseline commit is a reachable repository-context anchor.
The simulation proves the repository workflow can accept one bounded packet; it is not evidence of community adoption or independent review.
The example ticket contract is synthetic and must not be treated as a production support schema or data-handling policy.
nonGuarantees: This approval is not a security certification, vulnerability-free claim, community endorsement, or independent audit.
It does not claim public registry installation, live deployment, future compatibility, or suitability for a production tenant.
